youtube_items.title: [YT] The Most Incredible Transformation I’ve Ever Seen — Jerzy Gregorek on Cerebral Palsy and Coaching
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Jerzy Gregorek is a 4x World Weightlifting Champion, co-founder of UCLA’s weightlifting team, and co-creator, with his wife Aniela, of the Happy Body program. You can watch Prisoner No More for free here: https://tim.blog/hardchoices. To fill out the form on Cerebral Palsy Research Project, visit https://tim.blog/cp.

youtube_items.title: [YT] PRISONER NO MORE: The True Story of Tae Jin Park
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Prisoner No More follows Tae Jin Park, a young man diagnosed with cerebral palsy, who dismantled every physical limitation medical science predicted for him. Through athletic training under Olympian strength coach Jerzy Gregorek—and an uncompromising commitment to identity transformation—Tae Jin's story redefines what the human body and mind are capable of.

Learn more: https://wolfeprideproductions.com/

Directed By Jeff Wolfe
Starring Jerzy Gregorek & Tae Jin Park
Produced By Carole Middleton

youtube_items.title: [YT] My Current Longevity Stack
youtube_items.summary: [DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Elad Gil is CEO of Gil & Co, a multi-stage investment firm, holding company, and operating company working on the world’s most advanced technologies. Elad is a serial entrepreneur, operating executive, and investor or advisor to private companies, including AirBnB, Anduril, Coinbase, Figma, Instacart, OpenAI, SpaceX, and Stripe. He was previously VP of Corporate Strategy at Twitter and started mobile at Google. He was the founder and CEO of Mixerlabs and Color. Elad is the author of the bestsell

analysis.problem: A public health crisis involving the spread of infectious diseases such as Ebola, hantavirus, and nicotine-related health risks is affecting vulnerable populations globally.
analysis.root_cause: Lack of global health equity, limited regulatory oversight, and insufficient public health infrastructure in low-resource regions are enabling the spread of infectious diseases and the proliferation of harmful products like nicotine pouches.
leverage_points: Global health organizations like the WHO and national governments must strengthen regulatory frameworks and public health infrastructure.
Community-based health education and early intervention programs are critical to addressing preventable diseases and health risks.

proposed_actions.action: Expand WHO-led initiatives to improve disease surveillance and response in low-resource countries, with a focus on Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks.
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of new cases in affected regions by 30% within 12 months.

proposed_actions.action: Implement stricter regulations on nicotine product marketing, particularly targeting youth, with global coordination.
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Decrease youth nicotine pouch sales by 20% within 18 months.

evidence: Epidemic of Ebola Disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda determined a public health emergency of international concern.
WHO warns nicotine pouch brands targeting youth as sales surge.
analysis.generalization: This problem reflects broader systemic failures in global health governance, public health equity, and the regulation of health-related products, which impact both human well-being and the long-term sustainability of intelligent life on Earth.
analysis.summary: A critical public health crisis is emerging due to infectious disease outbreaks and harmful product proliferation, requiring urgent global action and regulatory reform.

raw_items.title: Epidemic of Ebola Disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda determined a public health emergency of international concern
raw_items.summary: Epidemic of Ebola Disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda determined a public health emergency of international concern

raw_items.title: WHO warns nicotine pouch brands targeting youth as sales surge
raw_items.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) warns about the rapid growth and aggressive marketing of nicotine pouches, especially targeting youth, amid limited regulation worldwide. Nicotine pouches pose significant health risks and governments are urged to implement strong regulatory measures to prevent addiction and protect young people.

raw_items.title: Follow-up message by the WHO Director-General to the people of Tenerife regarding the hantavirus response
raw_items.summary: A message from the Director-General of the World Health Organization expressing gratitude to the people of Tenerife for their solidarity and successful cooperation during the operation to safely disembark and care for over 120 passengers from 23 countries amid an outbreak of hantavirus.

raw_items.title: Tunisia validated by WHO as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem
raw_items.summary: Tunisia has been officially validated by the World Health Organization (WHO) as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, marking a significant achievement after decades of sustained effort.

raw_items.title: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
raw_items.summary: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

raw_items.title: 'We're right on track,' says Streeting as key target for hospital waiting times hit
raw_items.summary: Government his its interim target of 65% of patients in England being treated within 18 weeks.

raw_items.title: Posh sandwich has more salt than nearly five cheeseburgers
raw_items.summary: Action on Salt & Sugar said people should not be exposed to a "hidden health risk every time they buy lunch".

raw_items.title: MPs call for sunbed advertising ban to help prevent skin cancer
raw_items.summary: A report by a cross-party group of MPs has found the majority of skin cancer cases are preventable.
